title: OpenID Connect ID Token Claims
description: Schema describing the claims contained in an OpenID Connect ID Token as defined in the OIDC Core 1.0 specification. The ID Token is a JSON Web Token (JWT) that contains claims about the authentication event and the end-user. It is the primary artifact that distinguishes OIDC from plain OAuth 2.0.

iss.description: Issuer Identifier for the issuer of the response. Must be the HTTPS URL of the OpenID Provider.

sub.description: Subject Identifier. A locally unique and never reassigned identifier for the end-user within the issuer, intended to be consumed by the client.

aud.description: Audience(s) that this ID Token is intended for. Must contain the OAuth 2.0 client_id of the relying party.

exp.description: Expiration time on or after which the ID Token must not be accepted for processing. Represented as seconds since the Unix epoch.

iat.description: Time at which the JWT was issued. Represented as seconds since the Unix epoch.

auth_time.description: Time when the end-user authentication occurred. Required when a max_age request is made or when auth_time is requested as an essential claim.

nonce.description: A string value used to associate a client session with an ID Token and to mitigate replay attacks. The value is passed through unmodified from the authentication request.

acr.description: Authentication Context Class Reference. A string specifying the authentication context class that the authentication performed satisfied.

amr.description: Authentication Methods References. JSON array of strings that are identifiers for authentication methods used in the authentication.

azp.description: Authorized Party. The client_id of the party to which the ID Token was issued. Required when the ID Token has a single audience value that differs from the authorized party.

at_hash.description: Access Token hash value. Provides validation that the access token is tied to the ID Token.

c_hash.description: Code hash value. Provides validation that the authorization code is tied to the ID Token.

s_hash.description: State hash value. Provides validation that the state parameter is tied to the ID Token.

name.description: Full name of the end-user in displayable form.

given_name.description: Given name(s) or first name(s) of the end-user.

family_name.description: Surname(s) or last name(s) of the end-user.

preferred_username.description: Shorthand name by which the end-user wishes to be referred to.

profile.description: URL of the end-user's profile page.

picture.description: URL of the end-user's profile picture.

website.description: URL of the end-user's web page or blog.

email_verified.description: Whether the end-user's email address has been verified.

birthdate.description: End-user's birthday in ISO 8601 YYYY-MM-DD format. The year may be 0000 to indicate it was omitted.

zoneinfo.description: End-user's time zone from the IANA Time Zone Database.

locale.description: End-user's locale as a BCP 47 language tag.

phone_number.description: End-user's preferred telephone number in E.164 format.

phone_number_verified.description: Whether the end-user's phone number has been verified.

formatted.description: Full mailing address, formatted for display.

street_address.description: Full street address, which may include house number, street name, and PO box.

updated_at.description: Time the end-user's information was last updated. Represented as seconds since the Unix epoch.
